title: Pellaea striata

intro: Pellaea striata, also known as the striped cliff fern, is a species of fern native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its distinctive fronds with narrow, striated leaves. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in wound healing and the management of various infections. Pellaea striata contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine and is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, as well as in the form of a topical cream or salve, to harness its therapeutic benefits.
